name: NuScale Power Corporation

profile.description: NuScale Power is the first and only small modular reactor (SMR) technology company to receive U.S. NRC design approval, including the uprated 77 MWe NuScale Power Module. The company targets nuclear power for AI data centers, advanced manufacturing, and defense applications, with ENTRA1 as its exclusive global commercialization partner.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): First and only SMR to receive U.S. NRC design approval (2020), including the uprated 77 MWe module [1]. ENTRA1 reached collaborative agreement with TVA for up to 6 GW of NuScale SMR capacity. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI-specific deployment, though SMRs are positioned to power AI data centers. Market Share (2/2): Monopoly position as the only NRC-approved SMR technology; in discussions with majority of 'tier one' hyperscalers [2]. Pipeline (2/2): 6 GW TVA agreement, hyperscaler discussions, and up to 924 MWe per 12-module plant create massive addressable market. R&D Intensity (1/1): CTO Dr. José Reyes is the co-designer of the reactor technology, with deep technical innovation.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): NuScale's mission to 'solve today's energy problems for a better tomorrow' with carbon-free nuclear power represents a genuine and enduring Just Cause. Community Impact (1/2): Carbon-free power generation addresses climate change; community job creation potential from multi-GW deployment. Employee Retention (1/2): CEO John Hopkins has led since 2012, CTO Reyes since 2007 — exceptional leadership stability [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Long-tenured management team with significant personal investment in the mission.

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: NuScale has near-zero commercial revenue ($31.5M TTM, down 14.9% from reduced licensing fees), net loss of $355.8M, and EPS of -$2.17 [1][3]. The $495M payment to ENTRA1 in Q3 2025 triggered a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Forward P/E of -27.0 reflects deep pre-revenue status. However, liquidity surged 194% to $1.3B, providing substantial runway. Revenue is entirely dependent on future power purchase agreements that have not yet been signed. The stock is down 63% over six months. Liquidity floor: 25.8M avg volume x $11.44 = $295M daily turnover passes.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Nuclear power generation is a physical-world operation requiring NRC licensing, engineering expertise, and multi-year construction — AI cannot replicate or substitute for these capabilities. NuScale's NRC design approval creates a regulatory moat that would take competitors years to match [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): AI data center power demand is NuScale's primary growth catalyst — hyperscalers need reliable, carbon-free baseload power that SMRs can provide continuously, unlike intermittent renewables.

infinite_game.summary: NuScale demonstrates infinite game thinking through its 24-year journey from university research project to NRC-approved technology and CEO Hopkins's 14-year tenure. However, the ENTRA1 controversy ($495M payment triggering a securities fraud lawsuit) and lack of any signed power purchase agreements raise serious questions about execution and governance.

just_cause.analysis: NuScale's mission of providing carbon-free nuclear power to address climate change while meeting growing energy demand represents a compelling Just Cause that originated as a university research project in 2002 [2]. The 24-year pursuit of this vision demonstrates extraordinary long-term commitment.

trusting_teams.analysis: CEO Hopkins (14 years) and CTO Reyes (19 years) provide remarkable leadership continuity [2]. However, the ENTRA1 controversy — with allegations that the partner 'had never built, financed, or operated any significant project' — raises questions about due diligence and team judgment [3].

worthy_rivals.analysis: NuScale has no direct approved rivals in the SMR space, which could breed complacency. The cancelled Utah CFPP project (2023) demonstrated that monopoly position doesn't guarantee commercial success. X-energy and TerraPower represent emerging competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: NuScale has pivoted from utility-scale to AI data center power applications and from direct commercialization to the ENTRA1 partnership model. However, the ENTRA1 controversy suggests the pivot may have been poorly executed [3].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Pursuing nuclear technology commercialization for 24 years requires genuine courage, and NuScale's willingness to target the AI data center market shows forward thinking [2]. However, the $495M ENTRA1 payment and subsequent class action lawsuit suggest governance failures that undermine leadership credibility.

investment_thesis: NuScale holds the only NRC-approved SMR design globally and is in discussions with a majority of tier-one hyperscalers seeking carbon-free baseload power for AI data centers [2]. ENTRA1's 6 GW TVA agreement, if executed, would represent transformative revenue. Analyst consensus price target of $21 (median) implies 83% upside, with bulls at $60. Zacks projects 2026 revenue of $91.1M (202% growth). $1.3B in liquidity provides multi-year runway. The secular tailwind of nuclear renaissance for AI power demand could catalyze significant re-rating.
thesis_continued: The bear case is severe: NuScale has zero binding power purchase agreements, a securities fraud class action lawsuit over the $495M ENTRA1 payment, and $355.8M net losses on just $31.5M TTM revenue [1][3]. The ENTRA1 partner allegedly 'had never built, financed, or operated any significant project,' calling into question the company's primary commercialization strategy. Revenue declined 94.7% as licensing income dried up. The stock is down 63% in six months. Nuclear waste concerns, regulatory uncertainty, and the broader nuclear ESG debate create additional headwinds. Without a signed customer, NuScale's future remains entirely speculative.
key_catalysts: First binding power purchase agreement with a hyperscaler or utility would be the most significant de-risking event, proving commercial viability
ENTRA1 TVA 6 GW collaborative agreement progression from non-binding to binding commitments with construction timelines
Resolution of the securities fraud class action lawsuit removing legal uncertainty overhang from the stock
key_risks: Securities fraud class action lawsuit (April 20, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline) over $495M ENTRA1 payment creates significant legal and reputational risk
Zero binding power purchase agreements — all revenue projections depend on future contract execution that is not guaranteed
Nuclear waste management, water usage, and broader ESG concerns continue to limit institutional investor appetite for nuclear technology companies
sources: NuScale Power Q4 and FY2025 Financial Results, March 2026
NuScale Power leadership and hyperscaler discussions — Axios Pro, company website, 2025-2026
NuScale Power securities fraud class action filings — Morningstar, PR Newswire, March 2026
